az-001.attackPerspective: When "Enable MFA for all users" is not enforced, an attacker who gains initial access can exploit this identity gap to achieve complete environment compromise, data exfiltration, and persistent backdoor access. Identity misconfigurations are the #1 initial access vector in cloud breaches. Modern attack frameworks (MITRE ATT&CK T1078, T1098) specifically target identity weaknesses as the primary pivot point for cloud compromise. This is frequently seen in real-world incidents involving credential stuffing, token theft, and OAuth app abuse.
az-001.implementationNotes:
1. Access Admin Console with appropriate administrative credentials
2. Navigate to the configuration area for this control
3. Review the current state
4. Apply the recommended configuration
5. Validate the change
6. Document in change management
az-001.validationNotes:
# Verify compliance
# Azure CLI — check Conditional Access policies
az rest --method get --url "https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/identity/conditionalAccess/policies"
# Create via Portal:
# Entra ID → Security → Conditional Access → New Policy
# Target: All users | Conditions: All apps | Grant: Require MFA


# Expected: Output should confirm the control is properly configured.
# Re-run periodically or integrate into automated compliance scanning.
az-001.tuningNotes: This is a critical control — implement as-is without relaxation. Any exceptions must be formally risk-accepted by CISO or equivalent. Prioritize phishing-resistant MFA methods (FIDO2, Windows Hello for Business) over SMS/voice. For legacy systems that cannot support modern MFA, implement compensating controls and document risk acceptance.
az-001.advancedDetection: Monitor via Audit logs. Alert on configuration changes that deviate from the hardened baseline.

az-005.attackPerspective: When "Enable Microsoft Defender for Cloud" is not properly configured, attackers can operate without detection, leading to complete environment compromise, data exfiltration, and persistent backdoor access. Without proper logging, attackers operate undetected, extending dwell time from hours to months. In incident response, the absence of logs is the single biggest factor that extends investigation time and prevents accurate scoping of a breach.
az-005.implementationNotes:
1. Access Admin Console with appropriate administrative credentials
2. Navigate to the configuration area for this control
3. Review the current state
4. Apply the recommended configuration
5. Validate the change
6. Document in change management
az-005.validationNotes:
# Verify compliance
# Check Defender status
az security pricing list --query "[].{Name:name, Tier:pricingTier}" --output table
# Enable for key resources
az security pricing create --name VirtualMachines --tier Standard
az security pricing create --name SqlServers --tier Standard
az security pricing create --name StorageAccounts --tier Standard


# Expected: Output should confirm the control is properly configured.
# Re-run periodically or integrate into automated compliance scanning.
az-005.tuningNotes: This is a critical control — implement as-is without relaxation. Any exceptions must be formally risk-accepted by CISO or equivalent.
az-005.advancedDetection: Monitor via Audit logs. Alert on configuration changes that deviate from the hardened baseline.

az-002.attackPerspective: When "Disable legacy authentication protocols" is not enforced, an attacker who gains initial access can exploit this identity gap to achieve complete environment compromise, data exfiltration, and persistent backdoor access. Identity misconfigurations are the #1 initial access vector in cloud breaches. Modern attack frameworks (MITRE ATT&CK T1078, T1098) specifically target identity weaknesses as the primary pivot point for cloud compromise. This is frequently seen in real-world incidents involving credential stuffing, token theft, and OAuth app abuse.
az-002.implementationNotes:
1. Access Admin Console with appropriate administrative credentials
2. Navigate to the configuration area for this control
3. Review the current state
4. Apply the recommended configuration
5. Validate the change
6. Document in change management
az-002.validationNotes:
# Verify compliance
# Create Conditional Access policy to block legacy auth:
# Entra ID → Conditional Access → New Policy
# Conditions → Client apps → Select "Exchange ActiveSync" + "Other clients"
# Grant → Block access


# Expected: Output should confirm the control is properly configured.
# Re-run periodically or integrate into automated compliance scanning.
az-002.tuningNotes: This is a critical control — implement as-is without relaxation. Any exceptions must be formally risk-accepted by CISO or equivalent. Prioritize phishing-resistant MFA methods (FIDO2, Windows Hello for Business) over SMS/voice. For legacy systems that cannot support modern MFA, implement compensating controls and document risk acceptance.
az-002.advancedDetection: Monitor via Audit logs. Alert on configuration changes that deviate from the hardened baseline.

az-003.attackPerspective: When "Enable Privileged Identity Management (PIM)" is not enforced, an attacker who gains initial access can exploit this identity gap to achieve significant unauthorized access, privilege escalation, or data exposure. Identity misconfigurations are the #1 initial access vector in cloud breaches. Modern attack frameworks (MITRE ATT&CK T1078, T1098) specifically target identity weaknesses as the primary pivot point for cloud compromise. This is frequently seen in real-world incidents involving credential stuffing, token theft, and OAuth app abuse.
az-003.implementationNotes:
1. Access Admin Console with appropriate administrative credentials
2. Navigate to the configuration area for this control
3. Review the current state
4. Apply the recommended configuration
5. Validate the change
6. Document in change management
az-003.validationNotes:
# Verify compliance
# Azure CLI — list privileged role assignments
az role assignment list --query "[?roleDefinitionName=='Owner' || roleDefinitionName=='Contributor'].[principalName,roleDefinitionName]" --output table
# Configure PIM via Portal:
# Entra ID → PIM → Azure AD roles → Settings → Require justification + MFA


# Expected: Output should confirm the control is properly configured.
# Re-run periodically or integrate into automated compliance scanning.
az-003.tuningNotes: Customize thresholds based on your organization's risk profile. Test changes in a staging environment before production deployment. Document any deviations with formal risk acceptance.
az-003.advancedDetection: Monitor via Audit logs. Alert on configuration changes that deviate from the hardened baseline.

az-004.attackPerspective: Without "Configure Network Security Groups (NSGs)", the attack surface expands to allow external reconnaissance and potential exploitation leading to complete environment compromise, data exfiltration, and persistent backdoor access. Network exposure directly increases the attack surface visible to external threat actors. Threat actors routinely scan for exposed services (Shodan, Censys) and can discover misconfigurations within minutes of deployment.
az-004.implementationNotes:
1. Access Admin Console with appropriate administrative credentials
2. Navigate to the configuration area for this control
3. Review the current state
4. Apply the recommended configuration
5. Validate the change
6. Document in change management
az-004.validationNotes:
# Verify compliance
# List NSGs and rules
az network nsg list --query "[].{Name:name, RG:resourceGroup}" --output table
# Check for open rules
az network nsg rule list --nsg-name <nsg_name> --resource-group <rg> \
  --query "[?access=='Allow' && direction=='Inbound' && sourceAddressPrefix=='*']"


# Expected: Output should confirm the control is properly configured.
# Re-run periodically or integrate into automated compliance scanning.
az-004.tuningNotes: This is a critical control — implement as-is without relaxation. Any exceptions must be formally risk-accepted by CISO or equivalent.
az-004.advancedDetection: Monitor via Audit logs. Alert on configuration changes that deviate from the hardened baseline.

az-006.attackPerspective: When "Enable diagnostic logging for key resources" is not properly configured, attackers can operate without detection, leading to significant unauthorized access, privilege escalation, or data exposure. Without proper logging, attackers operate undetected, extending dwell time from hours to months. In incident response, the absence of logs is the single biggest factor that extends investigation time and prevents accurate scoping of a breach.
az-006.implementationNotes:
1. Access Admin Console with appropriate administrative credentials
2. Navigate to the configuration area for this control
3. Review the current state
4. Apply the recommended configuration
5. Validate the change
6. Document in change management
az-006.validationNotes:
# Verify compliance
# Enable Activity Log export
az monitor diagnostic-settings create --name "scw-audit" \
  --resource "/subscriptions/<sub_id>" \
  --workspace "<log_analytics_workspace_id>" \
  --logs '[{"category":"Administrative","enabled":true},{"category":"Security","enabled":true},{"category":"Alert","enabled":true}]'


# Expected: Output should confirm the control is properly configured.
# Re-run periodically or integrate into automated compliance scanning.
az-006.tuningNotes: Tune log retention based on regulatory requirements: GDPR (varies), HIPAA (6 years), PCI-DSS (1 year), SOC2 (1 year). Forward to SIEM for correlation and alerting. Consider cost optimization by tiering storage (hot → cool → archive).
az-006.advancedDetection: Monitor via Audit logs. Alert on configuration changes that deviate from the hardened baseline.

az-007.attackPerspective: Without "Enable Azure Disk Encryption", sensitive data can be intercepted or extracted, resulting in significant unauthorized access, privilege escalation, or data exposure. Missing encryption exposes data to interception, extraction, and regulatory penalties. Regulatory frameworks (GDPR, HIPAA, PCI-DSS) mandate encryption controls, making this both a security and compliance requirement.
az-007.implementationNotes:
1. Access Admin Console with appropriate administrative credentials
2. Navigate to the configuration area for this control
3. Review the current state
4. Apply the recommended configuration
5. Validate the change
6. Document in change management
az-007.validationNotes:
# Verify compliance
# Check encryption status
az vm encryption show --resource-group <rg> --name <vm_name>
# Enable on VM
az vm encryption enable --resource-group <rg> --name <vm_name> \
  --disk-encryption-keyvault <keyvault_name> --volume-type All


# Expected: Output should confirm the control is properly configured.
# Re-run periodically or integrate into automated compliance scanning.
az-007.tuningNotes: Ensure TLS 1.2+ is enforced. For internal systems with legacy dependencies, create an exception register with remediation timeline. Customer-managed keys provide additional control but increase operational complexity.
az-007.advancedDetection: Monitor via Audit logs. Alert on configuration changes that deviate from the hardened baseline.

az-008.attackPerspective: Without "Enforce HTTPS on storage accounts", sensitive data can be intercepted or extracted, resulting in complete environment compromise, data exfiltration, and persistent backdoor access. Missing encryption exposes data to interception, extraction, and regulatory penalties. Regulatory frameworks (GDPR, HIPAA, PCI-DSS) mandate encryption controls, making this both a security and compliance requirement.
az-008.implementationNotes:
1. Access Admin Console with appropriate administrative credentials
2. Navigate to the configuration area for this control
3. Review the current state
4. Apply the recommended configuration
5. Validate the change
6. Document in change management
az-008.validationNotes:
# Verify compliance
# Check all storage accounts
az storage account list --query "[].{Name:name, HTTPS:enableHttpsTrafficOnly}" --output table
# Enable on specific account
az storage account update --name <account> --resource-group <rg> --https-only true


# Expected: Output should confirm the control is properly configured.
# Re-run periodically or integrate into automated compliance scanning.
az-008.tuningNotes: This is a critical control — implement as-is without relaxation. Any exceptions must be formally risk-accepted by CISO or equivalent.
az-008.advancedDetection: Monitor via Audit logs. Alert on configuration changes that deviate from the hardened baseline.
